title: High-Protein Paneer Salad
tagline: Pan-roasted Schezwan paneer over massaged kale and lettuce with a tangy Greek-yogurt dressing.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients (300 g paneer split across two + Greek yogurt dressing + veg + nuts). Strong protein-to-calorie ratio and low carb — a proper cutting-phase salad.
Leaner: use low-fat paneer to drop ~150 kcal per serving and minimise the pan oil.
Protein boost: swap the roasted peanuts for roasted chana (leaner) or add 50 g soaked, squeezed soya chunks pan-roasted alongside the paneer for +~15 g protein across the pair without much fat.
The Greek-yogurt-based dressing already adds protein — if you want it creamier and higher-protein still, use hung curd instead of oil in the dressing.

items: 300 g paneer, cut into medium cubes
1 tsp curd (optional, helps the coating cling)
Salt, a tiny pinch (sauce is already salty)
A squeeze of lemon or splash of pickle brine, to finish
2-3 tbsp roasted peanuts or roasted chana
Pinch of salt + few drops lemon (for massaging the kale)

steps.title: Marinate the paneer
steps.body: Toss the 300 g paneer cubes with 2-3 tsp Schezwan sauce, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1/4 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp curd and just a tiny pinch of salt. Rest 5-10 minutes. Cut them medium, not small, so they don't dry out.

steps.title: Massage the kale
steps.body: Put the 2 cups chopped kale in a bowl with a pinch of salt and a few drops of lemon, and massage it with your hands for 1-2 minutes until it softens and darkens. This actually matters — raw un-massaged kale is bitter and tough.

steps.title: Pan-roast the paneer
steps.body: Heat 1-2 tsp oil in a pan on medium-high. Add the paneer and let it sit undisturbed so one side crisps before flipping — aim for golden edges and a little char. If it releases water your heat is too low; if the sauce burns it's too high or added too early. Finish with a squeeze of lemon or splash of pickle brine (and a tiny pinch MSG if you want the restaurant hit).

steps.title: Mix the dressing
steps.body: Whisk 6 tbsp Greek yogurt + 2 tsp mustard + 1-2 tsp lemon juice + 1 tsp pickle brine + black pepper (and an optional tiny pinch garlic powder). Keep it clean — the paneer is doing the spicy heavy lifting, so no Schezwan in the dressing. Taste before adding salt; the brine already has some.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Bed of massaged kale + lettuce. Scatter the sliced capsicum and cucumber. Pile the hot Schezwan paneer on top, add the roasted peanuts/chana, then the sliced gherkins last so they stay crunchy. Drizzle the dressing and toss lightly — don't overmix once the paneer is in.

notes: Keep the roles separate: paneer = heat + umami + fat, dressing = cool + tang, gherkins = sharp crunch. That layering is what makes it taste composed instead of a flat mixed bowl.
Add gherkins and dressing at the very end to keep everything crisp. If it gets too sour, fix it with fat (oil/yogurt), not sugar.
Garlic powder belongs in the paneer marinade (binds to the surface, survives the heat), not dumped raw into the dressing where it turns pungent.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
